Lost in the Mountains? Take Notes Without an AI 'Cloud' Connection

User travels to remote areas and finds that 'AI-first' apps often fail or hang when they can't reach their servers for 'indexing'.

The Solution

NoAIMD is 100% functional offline. Your search, your links, and your files are all local. No 'Checking for AI updates' ever.

The Problem

User travels to remote areas and finds that 'AI-first' apps often fail or hang when they can't reach their servers for 'indexing'.

In today's digital landscape, it feels like every software company is rushing to integrate AI into their products, regardless of whether it actually adds value.

The NoAIMD Solution

NoAIMD is 100% functional offline. Your search, your links, and your files are all local. No 'Checking for AI updates' ever.

NoAIMD was built with a simple philosophy: focus on what matters. We believe that a markdown editor should be fast, reliable, and respect your privacy.

Technical Specifications

Startup Time: <100ms
Memory Usage: <5MB RAM
CPU Usage: <1% idle
Bundle Size: 87KB gzipped
Offline Capable: Yes
Data Encryption: AES-256 (local)
API Calls: 0 (100% offline)
Tracking Scripts: 0
Cloud Dependencies: None
Browser Support: Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+, Edge 90+
Markdown Compliance: 100% GFM compatible
Export Formats: MD, HTML, PDF, TXT

Key Features

  • Zero latency - opens in under 100ms
  • 100% private - your data never leaves your device
  • No AI bloat - no unnecessary background processes
  • Simple, clean interface - focus on your writing
  • Markdown support - full compatibility with standard Markdown syntax
  • Export options - save your work in multiple formats

Opens in under 100ms. No AI bloat slowing you down.

100% Private

Your data never leaves your device. No cloud, no tracking.

START WRITING NOW

⚠️ Pitfall Guide

As a senior developer with 15+ years of experience, I must warn you: Mainstream Markdown editors contain serious performance traps and privacy risks that most users are unaware of.

Pitfall 1: AI Features Cannot Be Truly Disabled
Notion and Obsidian's AI features continue running in the background even after being "disabled," consuming 200-400MB of RAM for "silent indexing" operations that you never authorized.
Pitfall 2: Cloud Sync Privacy Risks
Your note content is sent to third-party servers for analysis. Even with "end-to-end encryption," metadata (file sizes, edit timestamps, IP addresses) remains fully exposed to the service provider.
Pitfall 3: Plugin Security Vulnerabilities
Obsidian's third-party plugins have full system access permissions. Multiple incidents have occurred where plugins exfiltrated user data, including sensitive personal and business information.
Pitfall 4: Subscription Traps
"Free" tiers typically limit you to 3-5 devices and bombard you with AI feature upgrade popups every 15-20 minutes, actively disrupting your writing flow and productivity.
